sections.label: Section 982
sections.original: ποῖ γὰρ μολεῖν μοι δυνατὸν , ἐς ποίους βροτοὺς ,
sections.translation: — you have cause to mourn .

sections.label: Section 983
sections.original: τοῖς σοῖς ἀρήξαντ ’ ὲν πόνοισι μηδαμοῦ ;
sections.translation: Yes , Teucer , far too rash .

sections.label: Section 985
sections.original: δἐξαιτ ’ ἂν εὐπρόςωπος , ἵλεως τ ’ ἴσως ,
sections.translation: He is alone near the tent .

sections.label: Section 990
sections.original: τὸν δειλίᾳ προδόντα καὶ κακανδρίᾳ
sections.translation: Yes , while still alive , Teucer , Ajax ordered you to care for the child , just as you are in fact doing .

sections.label: Section 992
sections.original: κράτη θανόντος καὶ δόμους νέμοιμι σοὺς .
sections.translation: This sight is truly most painful to me of all that my eyes have seen .

sections.label: Section 995
sections.original: τέλος δ ’ ἀπωστὸς γῆς ἀποῤῥιφθήσομαι ,
sections.translation: And the journey truly loathsome to my heart above all other journeys is this one that I have just now made while pursuing and scouting out your footsteps , dearest Ajax , once I learned of your fate ! For a swift rumor about you , as if sent from some god , passed throughout all the Greek army , telling that you were dead and gone .

sections.label: Section 1000
sections.original: οἴ μοι , τί δράσω ; πῶς σ ’ ἀποσπάσω πικροῦ
sections.translation:
I heard the rumor while still far away from you , and I groaned quietly in sadness . But now that I see its truth , my heart is utterly shattered ! Oh , god !
Come , uncover him ; let me see the worst . The corpse of Ajax is uncovered .
O face painful to look upon and full of cruel boldness ,

sections.label: Section 1005
sections.original: Ἕκτωρ μὲν , ᾧ δὴ τοῦδ ’ ἐδωρήθη πάρα ,
sections.translation: what a full crop of sorrows you have sown for me in your death ! Where can I go ? What people will receive me , when I have failed to help you in your troubles ? No doubt Telamon , your father and mine , will likely greet me with a smile and kind words ,

sections.label: Section 1010
sections.original: ἆρ ’ οὐκ Ἐριννὺς τοῦτ ’ ἐχάλκευσε ξίφος ,
sections.translation: when I return without you . Yes , of course he will — a man who , even when enjoying good fortune , tends not to smile more brightly than before ! What will a man like him leave unsaid ? What insult will he forego against the bastard offspring of his spear ’ s war-prize , against your cowardly , unmanly betrayer , dear Ajax ,

sections.label: Section 1015
sections.original: κεῖνος τὰ κείνου στεργέτω , κἀγὼ τάδε .
sections.translation: or better yet , your treacherous betrayer with designs to govern your domain and your house after your death ? So will he insult me ; he is a man quick to anger , severe in old age , and his rage seeks quarrels without cause . And in the end I shall be thrust out of our land , and cast off ,

sections.label: Section 1020
sections.original: Τίς δ ’ ἔστιν , ὅντιν ’ ἄνδρα προςλεύσσεις στρατοῦ ;
sections.translation: branded by his taunts as a slave instead of a freeman . These are my prospects at home . At Troy , on the other hand , my enemies are many , while I have few things to help me . All this have I gained from your death ! Ah , me , what shall I do ? How shall I draw your poor corpse

sections.label: Section 1025
sections.original: Τινος χάριν τοσόνδ ’ ἀνάλωσας λόγον ;
sections.translation:
off the sharp tooth of this gleaming sword , the murderer who , it seems , made you breathe your last ? Now do you see how in time Hector , though dead , was to destroy you ?
By the gods , note the fortune of this mortal pair .

sections.label: Section 1030
sections.original: ἐξεύρομεν ζητοῦντες ἐχθίω Φρυγῶν .
sections.translation: First Hector with the very warrior ’ s belt given to him by Ajax was lashed to the chariot-rail and shredded without end , until his life fled with his breath . Now Ajax here had this gift from Hector , and by this he has perished in his deadly fall . Was it not the Fury who forged this blade ,

sections.label: Section 1035
sections.original: θανόντες ἂν προὐκείμεθ ’ αἰσχίστῳ μόρῳ ·
sections.translation: was not that belt the product of Hades , the grim artificer ? I , for my part , would affirm that these happenings and all happenings ever are designed by the gods for men . But if there is anyone in whose judgment my words are unacceptable , let him cherish his own thoughts , as I do mine .

sections.label: Section 1040
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ ἀμφὶ χλωρὰν ψάμαθον ἐκβεβλημένος
sections.translation: Do not go on at length , but consider how you will bury him and what you will next say . For I see our enemy approaching , and chances are that he comes to mock at our sorrows , like one who would do us harm .

sections.label: Section 1044
sections.original: πάντως θανόντος γ ’ ἄρξομεν , κἂν μὴ θέλῃς ,
sections.translation: What man of the army do you see ?

sections.label: Section 1045
sections.original: χερσὶν παρευθύνοντες , οὐ γὰρ ἔσθ ’ ὅπου
sections.translation: Menelaus , the beneficiary of this expedition .

sections.label: Section 1046
sections.original:
1070
λόγων ἀκοῦσαι ζῶν ποτ ’ ἠθέλησ ’ ἐμῶν .
sections.translation: I see him ; he is not hard to recognize when near .

sections.label: Section 1047
sections.original: καίτοι κακοῦ πρὸς ἀνδρὸς , ἄνδρα δημότην
sections.translation: You there , I tell you not to lift that corpse for burial , but leave it where it lies .

sections.label: Section 1049
sections.original: οὐ γάρ ποτ ’ οὔτ ’ ἂν ὲν πόλει νόμοι καλῶς
sections.translation: Why do you waste your breath on this arrogant command ?

sections.label: Section 1050
sections.original: φέροιντ ’ ἂν ἔνθα μὴ καθεστήκῃ δέος ·
sections.translation: It conveys my decree , and the decree of the army ’ s supreme ruler .

sections.label: Section 1051
sections.original:
1075
οὔτ ’ ἂν στρατός γε σωφρόνως ἄρχοιτ ’ ἔτι ,
sections.translation: Would you mind , then , telling me what reason you pretend ?

sections.label: Section 1052
sections.original: μηδέν φόβου πρόβλημα μηδ ’ αἰδοῦς ἔχων .
sections.translation: This — that when we had hoped we were bringing Ajax from home to be an ally and a friend for the Greeks , we found him on closer examination to be an enemy worse than the Phrygians ,

sections.label: Section 1055
sections.original: δέος γὰρ ᾦ πρόςεστιν αἰσχύνη θ ’ ὁμοῦ ,
sections.translation: since he plotted the murder of the entire army and marched by night against us in order to take us with his spear . And if some god had not smothered this attempt , we would have been allotted the fate which he now has , and we would be dead and lie prostrate by an ignoble doom ,

sections.label: Section 1060
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ ἑστάτω μοι καὶ δέος τι καίριον ,
sections.translation:
while he would be living . But now a god has turned his outrage aside , so that it fell on the sheep and cattle .
For this reason there is no man so powerful that he will be able to entomb the corpse of Ajax . Instead he shall be cast forth somewhere on the yellow sand

sections.label: Section 1065
sections.original: καὶ σοὶ προφωνῶ τόνδε μὴ θάπτειν , ὅπως
sections.translation: to become forage for the birds of the seashore . So then do not inflame the terrible force of your spirit . If we were unable to master him while he lived , in any case in death , at least , we shall rule him despite your opposition and control him by force of our hands . For while he lived , there never was a time

sections.label: Section 1070
sections.original: ὅς μηδὲν ὢν γοναῖσιν εἶθ ’ ἁμαρτάνει ,
sections.translation:
when he would obey my commands .
Now it is , in truth , the mark of a base nature when a commoner does not think it right to obey those who stand over him . Never can the laws maintain a prosperous course in a city where fear has no fixed place ,

sections.label: Section 1075
sections.original: οὐκ αὐτὸς ἐξέπλευσεν , ὡς αὑτοῦ κρατῶν ;
sections.translation: nor can a camp be ruled any more with moderation , if it lacks the guarding force of fear and reverence . A man , though he grow his body great and mighty , must expect to fall , even from a light blow . Whoever knows fear and shame both ,

sections.label: Section 1080
sections.original: ἀρχῆς ἔκειτο θεσμὸς , ἢ καὶ τῷδε σέ .
sections.translation:
you can be certain that he has found his salvation ; but where there is license to attack others and act at will , do not doubt that such a State , though she has run before a favoring wind , will eventually sink with time into the depths .
No , let me see fear , too , established , where fear is fitting ;

sections.label: Section 1085
sections.original: εἴθ ’ ἅτερος στρατηγὸς εἰς ταφὰς ἐγὼ
sections.translation: let us not think that we can act on our desires without paying the price in pain . These things come by turns . He was once the hot attacker , now it is my hour to glory . And so I warn you not to bury him ,

sections.label: Section 1090
sections.original: σοῦ δ ’ οὐδέν · οὐ γὰρ ἠξίου τοὺς μηδένας .
sections.translation: so that you can avoid falling into your own grave .

sections.label: Section 1091
sections.original:
1115
πρὸς ταῦτα πλείοις δεῦρο κήρυκας λαβὼν
sections.translation: Menelaus , after laying down wise precepts , do not then violate the dead .

sections.label: Section 1093
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν στραφείην , ἔς τ ’ ἂν ᾖς , οἷός περ εἶ ,
sections.translation: Never again , my fellow Salaminians , will I be amazed if some nobody by birth does wrong ,

sections.label: Section 1095
sections.original: τὰ σκληρὰ γάρ τοι , κἂν ὑπέρδικ ’ ᾖ , δάκνει .
sections.translation:
when those who are reputed to be born of noble blood employ such wrongful sentiments in their arguments .
Come , tell me from the first once more — do you really say that you brought Ajax here to the Greeks as an ally personally recruited by you ? Did he not sail of his own accord ? As his own master ?

sections.label: Section 1100
sections.original: Η γλῶσσά σου τὸν θυμὸν ὡς δεινὸν τρέφει .
sections.translation: On what grounds are you his commander ? On what grounds have you a right to kingship over the men whom he brought from home ? It was as Sparta ’ s king that you came , not as master over us . Nowhere was it established among your lawful powers that you should order him any more than he you .

sections.label: Section 1105
sections.original: μὴ νυν ἀτίμα θεοὺς , θεοῖς σεσωσμένος .
sections.translation:
You sailed here under the command of others , not as a supreme commander who might at any time exercise authority over Ajax .
No , rule the troops you rule , and use your reverend words to punish them ! But this man , whether you or the other general forbid it , I will lay

sections.label: Section 1110
sections.original: Μισοῦντ ’ ἐμίσει · καὶ σὺ τοῦτ ’ ἠπίστασο .
sections.translation: in the grave as justice demands , and I will not fear your tongue . It was not at all for your wife ’ s sake that Ajax made this expedition , as did those toil-worn drudges . No , it was for the sake of the oath by which he had sworn , and not at all for you , since it was not his habit to value nobodies .

sections.label: Section 1115
sections.original: Οὐ μᾶλλον , ὡς ἔοικεν , ἢ λυπήσομεν .
sections.translation: And so when you come here again , bring more heralds , and the leader of the expedition , too . Your bluster could not make me turn to notice you , so long as you are what you are .

sections.label: Section 1118
sections.original: Ἤδη ποτ ’ εἶδον ἄνδρ ἐγὼ γλώσσῃ θρασὺν ,
sections.translation: Again , I say , in these troubles I cannot approve of such a tone . Harsh words sting , however just they are .

sections.label: Section 1120
sections.original: ᾦ φθέγμ ’ ἂν οὐκ ἂν εὗρες , ὴνίκ ’ ἐκ κακῷ
sections.translation: The bowman seems to feel no little grandeur .

sections.label: Section 1121
sections.original:
1145
χειμῶνος εἴχετ ’, ἀλλ ’ ὑφ ’ εἵματος κρυφεὶς ,
sections.translation: I do , since it is no lowly skill that I possess .

sections.label: Section 1122
sections.original: πατεῖν παρεῖχε τῷ θέλοντι ναυτίλων .
sections.translation: How you would boast , if you had a shield !

sections.label: Section 1123
sections.original: οὕτω δὲ καὶ σὲ καὶ τὸ σὸν λάβρον στόμα ,
sections.translation: Even without a shield I would be a match for you fully armed .

sections.label: Section 1124
sections.original: σμικροῦ νέφους τάχ ’ ἄν τις ἐκπνεύοας μέγας
sections.translation: What a tongue you have ! What dreadful anger it feeds !

sections.label: Section 1125
sections.original: χειμὼν κατασβέσειε τὴν πολλὴν βοήν .
sections.translation: When right is with him , a man ’ s thoughts may be grand .

sections.label: Section 1126
sections.original:
1150
ἐγὼ δέ γ ’ ἄνδρ ’ ὄπωπα μωρίας πλέων ,
sections.translation: What , is it right that the man who murdered me should prosper ?

sections.label: Section 1127
sections.original: ὅς ὲν κακοῖς ὕβριζε τοῖσι τῶν πέλας .
sections.translation: Murdered you ? It is truly a strange happening , if in fact you live after being killed .

sections.label: Section 1128
sections.original: κἆτ ’ αὐτὸν εἰσιδών τις ἐμφερὴς ἐμοὶ ,
sections.translation: A god rescued me . So far as that corpse is concerned , I am in Hades .

sections.label: Section 1129
sections.original: ὀργήν θ ’ ὁμοῖος , εἶπε τοιοῦτον λόγον ·
sections.translation: Then since it was the gods who saved you , do not dishonor the gods .

sections.label: Section 1130
sections.original: Ἄνθρωπε , μὴ δρᾶ τοὺς τεθνηκότας κακῶς ·
sections.translation: What , would I find fault with divine law ?

sections.label: Section 1131
sections.original:
1155
εἰ γὰρ ποιήσεις , ἴσθι πημανούμενος .
sections.translation: Yes , if by your presence here you prevent burial of the dead .

sections.label: Section 1132
sections.original: τοιαῦτ ’ ἄνολβον ἄνδρ ’ ἐνουθέτει παρών .
sections.translation: Prevent it I do , since he was at war with me and I with him . Burial in such a case would not be right .

sections.label: Section 1133
sections.original: ὁρῶ δέ τοί νιν , κἄστιν , ὡς ἐμοὶ δοκεῖ ,
sections.translation: What do you mean ? Did Ajax ever stand forth publicly to war with you ?

sections.label: Section 1134
sections.original: οὐδείς ποτ ’ ἄλλος ἢ σύ · μῶν ᾐνιξάμην ;
sections.translation: He hated me as I hated him , and you knew it , too .

sections.label: Section 1135
sections.original: Ἄπειμι · καὶ γὰρ αἰσχρὸν , εἰ πύθοιτό τις ,
sections.translation: Yes , he hated you because you had been caught fixing the votes in order to rob him .

sections.label: Section 1136
sections.original:
1160
λόγοις κολάζειν , ᾧ βιάζεσθαι πάρα .
sections.translation: At the hands of the jurymen , not mine , he suffered that loss .

sections.label: Section 1137
sections.original: Ἄφερπέ νυν · κἀμοὶ γὰρ αἴσχιστον κλύειν
sections.translation: You could make a thousand stealthy crimes look pretty .

sections.label: Section 1138
sections.original: ἀνδρὸς ματαίου , φλαῦρ ’ ἔπη μυθουμένου .
sections.translation: That sentiment leads to pain for someone I know .

sections.label: Section 1139
sections.original: ἔσται μεγάλης ἔριδός τις ἀγών .
sections.translation: The pain will be no greater , I think , than that which we will inflict .

sections.label: Section 1140
sections.original: ἀλλ ’, ὡς δύνασαι , Τεῦκρε , ταχύνας
sections.translation: I will tell you once and for all — there is to be no burial for him .
